metadata.description: Managed-autonomy workflow for Claude Code: 11-step loop with audit/critique skills, four-lens parallel staff-review, diff-focused security + a11y reviews (with per-diff early-exit on docs-only PRs), agent-isolated planner + docs helpers, project-config-driven ship + ship-spike pipelines with stale-base preflight gate. v1.13.0 closes two failure-open gaps in the ship pipeline (FB-0062): a shared visual-significance predicate (lib/visual-significance.py, reused by verify-build + ship) makes BOTH visual deliverables — the ephemeral /flow:verify-build walkthrough (fresh for HEAD, >=1 captured frame) and a new visual-history.html entry referencing the branch — REQUIRED on a visually-significant change, their absence routing the PR to a DRAFT (ship Step 7a); /flow:verify-build makes capture mandatory there (zero captured frames => Unknown, never PASS) and ship Step 5c's distill no longer fails open on a short-circuited/grounding-less buffer (a hand-authored entry becomes the required path); and a NEW /flow:audit-skips skill (ship Step 2a, after the four reviewers, backed by the deterministic lib/skip-audit-checks.py) audits every stage skip for legitimacy — a skip is trusted only if the diff/config backs it and a 'ran' claim only if its canonical output artifact EXISTS for HEAD (verdict-without-artifact == skip), so a self-certified short-circuit routes to a draft instead of a silent ready PR. New run_visual_significance_evals.py + run_skip_audit_evals.py wired into CI. v1.12.0 adds /flow:land — a post-merge, human-invoked doc-currency skill (Claude can't merge, so it can't live in /flow:ship): after you merge a PR it verifies the merge (fail-loud otherwise, edits nothing), flips the item to 'merged (#N)' across roadmap/plan/history + moves it to Recently shipped, re-runs the §5c visual-history distill if a visual pass blocked at ship has since completed, clears reserved FB/VH numbers, checks CHANGELOG currency (stdlib lib/land-helpers.py), and opens a small `docs: land #N` PR — never merges (disable-model-invocation:true), idempotent on re-run. Closes the 'at PR → merged never reconciles' gap. Pinned by evals/run_land_evals.py, wired into CI (FB-0061). v1.11.1 extends the canonical `gh`-resilience fallback (the v1.10.1 Projects-classic `projectCards` deprecation workaround) to `/flow:ship-spike`'s PR-OPEN re-ship path — the one PR-write site v1.10.1 missed (FB-0010 fan-out) — and de-stales the `/flow:staff-review` §1.5 gh-safety note (FB-0060). v1.11.0 makes flow learn from itself and contribute back automatically (FB-0059): /flow:ship Step 4c harvests flow-generalizable lessons (reviewer false-positives, gate misfires, taste calls the human overruled) behind a ~free pre-scan cost gate, routes project-local vs flow-upstream through a deterministic noise+confidence filter, and enqueues to a user-scope cross-project queue; /flow:contribute (run from the flow checkout via flowRepoPath) drains that queue AND the previously-manual log-disagreement store, sanitizes out personal-project tokens (fail-closed, no shipped brand literals), and opens a DRAFT PR back to flow — self-triggered by a flow-repo SessionStart hook / local OS job, calibrating from prior PR outcomes; the human still gates the merge. Four new slots (flowRepoPath, contributionsQueuePath, lastHarvestedPath, contributionThreshold); pinned by run_contribution_evals.py in CI. v1.10.2 fixes a jq boolean-slot footgun: `jq '.X // true'` treats an explicit `false` as empty and falls through to the default, so `verifyEnabled: false` (and `uiSurface: false`) were silently inverted to enabled — the /flow:verify-build skip-gate ran despite the opt-out. Fixed all four sites (doctor, verify-build ×2, ship §5c) with the `if .X == false` form; FB-0058 names the grep-on-new-boolean-slot-read discipline (PR #44, brought current). v1.10.1 is a docs-only follow-up to v1.10.0: it documents a `gh` Projects-classic PR-write resilience fallback in /flow:ship + /flow:staff-review Step 7 — REST `PATCH` for the PR body + markPullRequestReadyForReview/convertPullRequestToDraft GraphQL mutations — for the `projectCards` deprecation that breaks `gh pr edit`/`ready`/`view` on classic-projects repos (the secondary item from the same FB-0056 dogfood report). v1.10.0 closes two dogfound verify-build/ship integrity holes (FB-0056): a per-criterion `provenance` field (adversarial-judged | spike-rubric | hand-authored; absent ⇒ hand-authored) makes the PR Test plan + HTML report render a HAND-AUTHORED pass as a distinct `[~]` self-report state + banner, never a forgeable machine `[x]`; /flow:verify-build Step 2 grants spike's reduced rigor ONLY to an explicit /flow:ship-spike (a missing plan is the no-plan fallback — source-touching → full judged path over diff-derived criteria + draft-routes, never a silent spike downgrade); and /flow:ship Step 1.0a gates source-touching diffs on a commit-invariant /flow:staff-review marker (stdlib lib/rigor-marker.py) as mechanical evidence /simplify + staff-review ran. New report-render + rigor-marker evals wired into CI. v1.9.1 hardens /flow:verify-build's visual-capture routing (V2.1): §5a now gates on its own predicate (uiSurface + a Visual-walk block via the new extract-visual-states.py parser) so a malformed **Spec-walk:** heading no longer silently skips the HTML visual summary; both plan parsers gain robust heading matching + active-(first-)block scoping via a shared walk_extract.py, retiring the author-memory 'qualify retained headings' convention for 'active PR plan at the top' (FB-0055). v1.9.0 adds the `statusDocs` slot — project-declared forward-looking status surfaces (e.g. a CLAUDE.md / README status line a cold agent reads) that /flow:ship reconciles every ship, beyond the built-in plan 'Current Focus' + roadmap 'Now'. Each entry declares a file + marker; flow edits only the fenced region `<!-- {marker} -->` … `<!-- /{marker} -->` (narrow + mechanical, never a restructure). Step 5b adds a version-manifest-INDEPENDENT marker-coverage gate that BLOCKS a ship which moved plan/roadmap status forward but left a declared region untouched (so non-versioned projects get real enforcement, not just a version-token check); /flow:doctor Check 2.7 verifies each surface is fenced. Default [] → no change for non-adopters (FB-0054). v1.8.1 fixes a dogfound bug in V3b's Step 5c distill: the asset-copy doubled the `assets/` path (`.../assets/assets/<frame>`) so the durable record's screenshots resolved to missing files — §5c now resolves frame sources against the report dir and copies by basename (aligning the §5a/§5c convention), pinned by a new eval guard; also clarifies resolved-this-iteration open-question routing. Caught by the first real health-tracker iOS cold-run (FB-0016), which otherwise confirmed §5c fires, the entry is curated + editorially sound, and screenshots load. v1.8.0 adds the durable visual record (Deliverable-quality track V3b): a `visualHistoryPath` slot + a /flow:ship Step 5c distill step that, on UI projects, turns a run's load-bearing visual decisions (the verify-build buffer's `grounding` + resolved this-iteration `open_questions`) into ONE curated, reverse-chronological entry in a committed `visual-history.html` — the picture companion to the history doc — with lean asset refs (CSS/SVG reconstruction fallback) via the stdlib `lib/insert-visual-history.py` (seeds from a bundled skeleton on first write; no bootstrap scaffold, so non-UI projects never get an empty doc). The ephemeral per-run report stays ephemeral (distill-then-discard). Heavily gated: self-skips with an explicit reason on uiSurface:false, a skipped verify-build, or a run with no load-bearing visual decision (curated, not a per-PR dump). Schema now 28 slots; entry shape provisional pending a UI cold-run (flow's own repo is uiSurface:false, so it always self-skips). v1.5.3 makes the PR `## Test plan` a non-forgeable projection of the /flow:verify-build findings buffer — /flow:ship Step 7 runs skills/ship/lib/render-test-plan.py to render one checkbox per Spec-walk criterion whose state IS the machine aggregated_verdict (PASS → checked, with the adversarial fresh-context judge's two-quote evidence; FAIL/Unknown → unchecked, with the judge's reason), plus the not_tested[] checklist — so the human confirms-and-merges instead of re-verifying, and the agent cannot show a criterion green without a real PASS. Skip / no-buffer / stale-buffer (buffer branch+sha ≠ current HEAD) / malformed → an honest 'no behavioral gate ran (reason); manual verification required' fallback, never a forged or stale render (so a platform:library repo — including flow's own — always takes the fallback). Renderer behavior pinned by evals/run_render_evals.py; attests behavioral/text verification only (not visual — Deliverable-quality V2) and only over DECLARED Spec-walk criteria (under-declaration coverage is a queued follow-up). v1.5.2 makes doc-currency automatic in the ship pipeline — /flow:ship Step 5a reconciles roadmap 'Now' + plan 'Current Focus' to the shipped version (Recently-shipped line, next-up pointer, FB-reservation clear) and Step 5b mechanically blocks any ship whose forward-looking docs don't reference the current version (enforced in-pipeline, NOT via manual /flow:doctor, which keeps a secondary Check 2.6 mirror); also corrects docs/upgrade.md (one `/plugin marketplace update` command updates the installed plugin, and autoUpdate automates it). v1.5.1 adds a `Visual-walk` plan field — declared, checkable visual/UX acceptance criteria a plan states for UI changes (gated on the uiSurface slot; N/A under spike/tiny), giving the Step 8/9 visual dial-in a declared bar and closing the workflow.md 'declared visual criteria' dangling reference; declaration-only. v1.5.0 adds ship-time gate semantics: security/a11y BLOCKERs carry a resolution-confidence tag ([auto-fixable] | [decision-required], default decision-required when unsure); decision-required findings and non-converging verify-build regressions route to a draft PR + a pinned NOT READY TO MERGE manifest (never a silent proceed, never a hard mid-loop halt), so an unresolved blocker reaches the human at the merge gate; verify-build at ship is reframed as a confirmation re-run (discovery moves to the Step 8/9 readiness boundary; visual sign-off folds into the merge gate). The v1.4.0 auto-advance predicate is unchanged (still requires a verify-build PASS; FB-0018 invariant intact); only ship-internal failure handling changed. It also makes the reviewer skills (/flow:audit-plan, /flow:audit-completion, /flow:critique-plan) and /flow:ship-spike model-invocable (disable-model-invocation false) — the only two human gates are plan approval and merge, and no review or ship-spike skill is itself a gate (superseding the v1.4.0 note that ship-spike stays manual); the context:fork reviewers rely on the v1.4.2 session-discovery fix to resolve worktree transcripts. v1.4.2 fixes a session-transcript discovery bug in extract_session.py — the cwd→project-dir encoding now matches Claude Code (all non-alphanumerics map to '-', not just '/') and discovery prefers the exact CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, so the audit/critique reviewers no longer silently run context-starved from worktree or dotted-path sessions. v1.4.1 makes /flow:ship + /flow:ship-spike write a per-step `## Flow run` PR body that documents the full loop run (each step ✓ ran / skipped-with-reason + any notable decision or finding; routine steps show '—', no manufactured filler), replacing the generic Reviews blurb. v1.4.0 makes /flow:ship auto-invocable: the agent auto-advances from Step 8 into the ship pipeline when the ship-readiness predicate holds (all spec-walk checkboxes checked, no open BLOCKER, no unresolved MEDIUM/LOW assumption, /flow:verify-build would return PASS) and the FB-0011 risk gate is clear — collapsing the autonomous loop to its two load-bearing human gates (plan approval, merge). Skipped-verify cases (library/none platform, doc-only diff) and spike mode stay manual; ship still never merges. v1.2.6 adds bounded-retry mechanical preflight (new `preflightCmd` slot; Step 1c of /flow:ship + /flow:ship-spike; N≤3 retries with diff-hash oscillation detection; loops only on externally-verifiable exit codes — reviewer outputs at Step 2 stay single-pass). v1.2.5 sharpens the reviewer pipeline along adversarial dimensions: auditor + plan-critic gain a prove-or-disprove self-check that drops findings without specific evidence; lens-staff-engineer reads adversarially ('assume the diff is broken'); /flow:security-review shifts to red-team identity ('find an exploitable vulnerability') with a trace-input-source disprove step.

plugins.name: flow
plugins.description: Managed-autonomy 11-step loop. Bundles the skeptical reviewers (auditor + plan-critic), the four-lens parallel staff-review (engineer / UX-designer / design-engineer / push-further), diff-focused /flow:security-review + /flow:accessibility-review, /flow:verify-build (runtime behavioral verification gate — v1.3.0), /flow:audit-coverage (under-declaration coverage auditor — v1.6.0), /flow:ship + /flow:ship-spike pipelines, /flow:workflow-help, /flow:doctor verification skill, /flow:land (post-merge doc-currency), planner + docs context-isolation agents, portable rules (general, plan-discipline, documentation, exploration), memory-corpus tool, flow.config.json JSON Schema (28 slots), and default hooks. v1.13.0 closes two failure-open gaps in the ship pipeline (FB-0062): a shared visual-significance predicate (lib/visual-significance.py, reused by verify-build + ship) makes BOTH visual deliverables — the ephemeral /flow:verify-build walkthrough (fresh for HEAD, >=1 captured frame) and a new visual-history.html entry referencing the branch — REQUIRED on a visually-significant change, their absence routing the PR to a DRAFT (ship Step 7a); /flow:verify-build makes capture mandatory there (zero captured frames => Unknown, never PASS) and ship Step 5c's distill no longer fails open on a short-circuited/grounding-less buffer (a hand-authored entry becomes the required path); and a NEW /flow:audit-skips skill (ship Step 2a, after the four reviewers, backed by the deterministic lib/skip-audit-checks.py) audits every stage skip for legitimacy — a skip is trusted only if the diff/config backs it and a 'ran' claim only if its canonical output artifact EXISTS for HEAD (verdict-without-artifact == skip), so a self-certified short-circuit routes to a draft instead of a silent ready PR. New run_visual_significance_evals.py + run_skip_audit_evals.py wired into CI. v1.12.0 adds /flow:land — a post-merge, human-invoked doc-currency skill (Claude can't merge, so it can't live in /flow:ship): after you merge a PR it verifies the merge (fail-loud otherwise, edits nothing), flips the item to 'merged (#N)' across roadmap/plan/history + moves it to Recently shipped, re-runs the §5c visual-history distill if a visual pass blocked at ship has since completed, clears reserved FB/VH numbers, checks CHANGELOG currency (stdlib lib/land-helpers.py), and opens a small `docs: land #N` PR — never merges (disable-model-invocation:true), idempotent on re-run. Closes the 'at PR → merged never reconciles' gap. Pinned by evals/run_land_evals.py, wired into CI (FB-0061). v1.11.1 extends the canonical `gh`-resilience fallback (the v1.10.1 Projects-classic `projectCards` deprecation workaround) to `/flow:ship-spike`'s PR-OPEN re-ship path — the one PR-write site v1.10.1 missed (FB-0010 fan-out) — and de-stales the `/flow:staff-review` §1.5 gh-safety note (FB-0060). v1.11.0 makes flow learn from itself and contribute back automatically (FB-0059): /flow:ship Step 4c harvests flow-generalizable lessons (reviewer false-positives, gate misfires, taste calls the human overruled) behind a ~free pre-scan cost gate, routes project-local vs flow-upstream through a deterministic noise+confidence filter, and enqueues to a user-scope cross-project queue; /flow:contribute (run from the flow checkout via flowRepoPath) drains that queue AND the previously-manual log-disagreement store, sanitizes out personal-project tokens (fail-closed, no shipped brand literals), and opens a DRAFT PR back to flow — self-triggered by a flow-repo SessionStart hook / local OS job, calibrating from prior PR outcomes; the human still gates the merge. Four new slots (flowRepoPath, contributionsQueuePath, lastHarvestedPath, contributionThreshold); pinned by run_contribution_evals.py in CI. v1.10.0 closes two dogfound verify-build/ship integrity holes (FB-0056): a per-criterion `provenance` field in the findings buffer (adversarial-judged | spike-rubric | hand-authored; absent ⇒ hand-authored) makes the PR Test plan (render-test-plan.py) + HTML report (render-report.py) render a HAND-AUTHORED pass as a distinct `[~]` self-report state + a 'not adversarially judged' banner instead of a forgeable machine `[x]` — a buffer the implementer wrote by hand can't masquerade as a fresh-context-judge verdict; /flow:verify-build Step 2 grants spike's reduced 3-check rigor ONLY to an explicit /flow:ship-spike (a missing/Spec-walk-less plan is the no-plan fallback — source-touching → the full judged path over diff-derived criteria + `no_plan_fallback` draft-routes the ship; docs-only → smoke), never a silent spike downgrade; and /flow:ship Step 1.0a gates source-touching diffs on a commit-invariant /flow:staff-review marker (stdlib lib/rigor-marker.py, written by staff-review, read by ship) as mechanical evidence /simplify + /flow:staff-review ran. New run_report_render_evals.py + run_rigor_marker_evals.py + a hand-authored render fixture, all wired into CI with the previously-orphaned run_visual_history_evals.py. v1.9.1 hardens /flow:verify-build's visual-capture routing (V2.1): §5a gates on its own predicate (uiSurface + a Visual-walk block via the new extract-visual-states.py parser) so a malformed **Spec-walk:** heading no longer silently skips the HTML visual summary; both plan parsers gain robust heading matching + active-(first-)block scoping via a shared walk_extract.py (FB-0055). v1.9.0 adds the `statusDocs` slot — project-declared forward-looking status surfaces (e.g. a CLAUDE.md / README status line) that /flow:ship reconciles every ship beyond the built-in plan 'Current Focus' + roadmap 'Now'; each entry declares a file + marker and flow edits only the fenced region `<!-- {marker} -->` … `<!-- /{marker} -->` (narrow + mechanical). Step 5a reconciles each region; Step 5b adds a version-manifest-INDEPENDENT marker-coverage gate that BLOCKS a ship moving plan/roadmap status forward while a declared region stays untouched; /flow:doctor Check 2.7 verifies each surface is fenced; backed by the stdlib lib/status-docs.py + evals/run_status_docs_evals.py. Default [] → no change for non-adopters (FB-0054). v1.8.1 fixes a dogfound bug in V3b's Step 5c distill: the asset-copy doubled the `assets/` path (`.../assets/assets/<frame>`) so the durable record's screenshots resolved to missing files — §5c now resolves frame sources against the report dir and copies by basename (aligning the §5a/§5c convention), pinned by a new eval guard; also clarifies resolved-this-iteration open-question routing. Caught by the first real health-tracker iOS cold-run (FB-0016), which otherwise confirmed §5c fires, the entry is curated + editorially sound, and screenshots load. v1.8.0 adds the durable visual record (Deliverable-quality track V3b): a `visualHistoryPath` slot + a /flow:ship Step 5c distill step that, on UI projects, turns a run's load-bearing visual decisions (the verify-build buffer's `grounding` + resolved this-iteration `open_questions`) into ONE curated, reverse-chronological entry in a committed `visual-history.html` — the picture companion to the history doc — with lean asset refs (CSS/SVG reconstruction fallback) via the stdlib `lib/insert-visual-history.py` (seeds from a bundled skeleton on first write; no bootstrap scaffold, so non-UI projects never get an empty doc). The ephemeral per-run report stays ephemeral (distill-then-discard). Heavily gated: self-skips with an explicit reason on uiSurface:false, a skipped verify-build, or a run with no load-bearing visual decision (curated, not a per-PR dump). Step 4a also derives a candidate FB from a human-corrected this-iteration open question. Completes V3 of the Deliverable-quality track; entry shape provisional pending a UI cold-run (flow's own repo is uiSurface:false → always self-skips). v1.7.1 is a plain-language copy pass on the /flow:verify-build report so a human understands it at a glance (clearer lede/legend, de-jargoned pills + observation labels; copy-only, from FB-0052). v1.7.0 makes the /flow:verify-build report a TWO-WAY review surface — when the buffer carries a captured frame, render-report.py injects a self-contained click-to-pin annotation overlay (lib/annotation-layer.html) so the human drops located pins on a screenshot and 'Copy notes' returns a structured per-screen block to paste back; a merge-gate visual flag re-enters Execute (mirroring an open_questions[this-iteration]). Completes V3a (the renderer shipped read-only in v1.6.0). Graceful: a frameless report stays read-only; an unreadable layer file warns, never crashes. v1.6.1 adds rendered visual capture + an ephemeral HTML walkthrough to /flow:verify-build (Deliverable-quality track V2/V3a): for each declared `Visual-walk` state, flow captures and persists a screenshot itself — a11y-gated (snapshot the a11y tree, assert the intended state, THEN screenshot), driving the platform's screenshot MCP (XcodeBuildMCP returns a native frame path on iOS; bundled /verify only narrates frames to the fresh-context judges) — plus an a11y_snapshot for text/status, writing path-referenced observations; two additive findings-buffer fields land (criteria[].grounding rationale + top-level open_questions; schema_version stays 1.0); the rubric judges visual claims pairwise-vs-baseline (no baseline ⇒ Unknown) and reads text from the a11y tree; a stdlib-Python renderer emits one self-contained HTML report — the artifact the human opens at the merge gate — with grounding callouts, per-dimension verdict cards, a standalone 'Open questions for you' block, and a 'what we did NOT test' checklist; an open_questions[this-iteration] entry blocks Step 8 auto-advance. New verifyReportPath slot. Validated by a cold skill-driven /flow:verify-build run on a real iOS surface (round 1 caught 3 §5a prose gaps, fixed + FB-0050; round 2 green). v1.6.0 adds /flow:audit-coverage — a coverage auditor closing the under-declaration hole the v1.5.3 Test-plan render left: it compares the workspace diff against the declared **Spec-walk:** criteria and flags behavior changes no criterion covers (a behavior /flow:verify-build never tested, so the rendered Test plan would be honestly all-green while the change ships unverified). Each gap routes to the draft manifest (decision-required) — PR mechanically NOT-READY until the criterion is declared + verified or the human waives it; never auto-added, never a hard halt. Reuses the auditor agent via a new 'Undeclared change' category (coverage mode) + extract-criteria.py; runs at the Step 8 readiness boundary + as the fourth /flow:ship Step 2 reviewer; runs on ALL platforms (does NOT skip on platform:library|none, unlike verify-build); self-skips on doc/test/refactor-only diffs or no **Spec-walk:** block. Best-effort LLM judgment — raises the completeness bar, does not deterministically guarantee it (false negatives possible). v1.5.3 makes the PR `## Test plan` a non-forgeable projection of the /flow:verify-build findings buffer — /flow:ship Step 7 runs skills/ship/lib/render-test-plan.py to render one checkbox per Spec-walk criterion whose state IS the machine aggregated_verdict (PASS → checked, with the adversarial fresh-context judge's two-quote evidence; FAIL/Unknown → unchecked, with the judge's reason), plus the not_tested[] checklist — so the human confirms-and-merges instead of re-verifying, and the agent cannot show a criterion green without a real PASS. Skip / no-buffer / stale-buffer (buffer branch+sha ≠ current HEAD) / malformed → an honest 'no behavioral gate ran (reason); manual verification required' fallback, never a forged or stale render (so a platform:library repo — including flow's own — always takes the fallback). Renderer behavior pinned by evals/run_render_evals.py; attests behavioral/text verification only (not visual — Deliverable-quality V2) and only over DECLARED Spec-walk criteria (under-declaration coverage is a queued follow-up). v1.5.2 makes doc-currency automatic in the ship pipeline — /flow:ship Step 5a reconciles roadmap 'Now' + plan 'Current Focus' to the shipped version (Recently-shipped line, next-up pointer, FB-reservation clear) and Step 5b mechanically blocks any ship whose forward-looking docs don't reference the current version (enforced in-pipeline, NOT via manual /flow:doctor, which keeps a secondary Check 2.6 mirror); also corrects docs/upgrade.md (one `/plugin marketplace update` command updates the installed plugin, and autoUpdate automates it). v1.5.1 adds a `Visual-walk` plan field — declared, checkable visual/UX acceptance criteria a plan states when the change has a UI surface (gated on the uiSurface slot; N/A under spike/tiny), giving the Step 8/9 visual dial-in a declared bar and closing the workflow.md 'declared visual criteria' dangling reference; declaration-only (mechanical verification is a later roadmap link). v1.5.0 adds ship-time gate semantics: security/a11y BLOCKERs carry a resolution-confidence tag ([auto-fixable] | [decision-required], default decision-required when unsure); decision-required findings and non-converging verify-build regressions route to a draft PR + a pinned NOT READY TO MERGE manifest (never a silent proceed, never a hard mid-loop halt), so an unresolved blocker reaches the human at the merge gate; verify-build at ship is reframed as a confirmation re-run (discovery moves to the Step 8/9 readiness boundary; visual sign-off folds into the merge gate). The v1.4.0 auto-advance predicate is unchanged (still requires a verify-build PASS; FB-0018 invariant intact); only ship-internal failure handling changed. It also makes the reviewer skills (/flow:audit-plan, /flow:audit-completion, /flow:critique-plan) and /flow:ship-spike model-invocable (disable-model-invocation false) — the only two human gates are plan approval and merge, and no review or ship-spike skill is itself a gate (superseding the v1.4.0 note that ship-spike stays manual); the context:fork reviewers rely on the v1.4.2 session-discovery fix to resolve worktree transcripts. v1.4.2 fixes a session-transcript discovery bug in extract_session.py — the cwd→project-dir encoding now matches Claude Code (all non-alphanumerics map to '-', not just '/') and discovery prefers the exact CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, so the audit/critique reviewers no longer silently run context-starved from worktree or dotted-path sessions. v1.4.1 makes /flow:ship + /flow:ship-spike write a per-step `## Flow run` PR body documenting the full loop run (each step ✓ ran / skipped-with-reason, mode- and config-dependent; Notable cell = genuine signal or '—', no manufactured filler; follow-ups stay canonical in roadmap/plan; PR still never merged), replacing the generic Reviews blurb. v1.4.0 makes /flow:ship auto-invocable (disable-model-invocation false): the agent auto-advances from Step 8 into ship when the ship-readiness predicate holds (spec-walk complete, no open BLOCKER, no unresolved MEDIUM/LOW assumption, verify-build PASS) and the FB-0011 risk gate is clear, else stops and presents; skipped-verify + spike stay manual; plan and merge gates untouched. v1.3.0 adds /flow:verify-build as the third final-pass reviewer at /flow:ship Step 2 (alongside security + a11y). Wraps bundled /verify with plan-driven criteria extraction from **Spec-walk:** checkboxes, fresh-context adversarial transformation, per-dimension parallel judges (PASS/FAIL/Unknown with two-citation evidence), Unknown-blocking gate per FB-0011, and structured findings buffer routed to /flow:ship Step 4a for FB-XXXX synthesis. Closes the static-analysis-only gap in the loop's verification surface (Potemkin-interface / hallucinated-success class no static reviewer catches). Inherits FB-0012 bounded-retry contract: judge runs single-pass; budget cap forces Unknown on overrun (mechanical exit signal — no judge-output loop); reward-hacking guards baked into adversarial.md. 4 new schema slots (platform / verifyEnabled / verifyFindingsPath / verifyBudgetCalls); ship-spike Step 2 spike-mode invocation; doctor Check 5.3 surfaces missing /run-skill-generator recipes. v1.2.6 adds bounded-retry mechanical preflight to /flow:ship + /flow:ship-spike (new `preflightCmd` slot at Step 1c; on non-zero exit, fix-and-retry up to N=3 invocations with diff-hash oscillation detection; loops ONLY on externally-verifiable exit codes per Anthropic's evaluator-optimizer guidance — reviewer outputs at Step 2 stay deliberately single-pass to prevent the reward-hacking failure mode that loops over LLM-judgment outputs invite). v1.2.5 sharpens the reviewer pipeline along adversarial dimensions: auditor + plan-critic gain a prove-or-disprove self-check (findings must survive an attempt to cite the specific session text or third citation that would invalidate them); lens-staff-engineer reads adversarially ('assume the diff is broken — what's the most likely break?') for higher correctness recall; /flow:security-review shifts to red-team identity ('find an exploitable vulnerability') with a trace-input-source-back disprove step. Plan-critic also gains fan-out hunt vocabulary in Internal incoherence (PR-G FOLLOW-UP #5). v1.2.3 adds consistency discipline (FB-0010): the lens-staff-engineer agent now explicitly hunts fan-out contradictions (counts/names/slots referenced in N places, only some updated) and silent-skip patterns (failures swallowed via `2>/dev/null` / unset-fallback / regex inversion); /flow:doctor gains Check 2.5 comparing documented slot counts against the schema source-of-truth; workflow.md Step 4 names the discipline.
